source_name: Code4rena Ethena Findings and Analysis Report (2023-10)
finding_title: Soft Restricted Staker Role can withdraw stUSDe for USDe
summary: A requirement is stated that a user with the SOFT_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE is not allowed to withdraw USDe for stUSDe.
root_cause: Report-described logic/accounting flaw: A requirement is stated that a user with the SOFT_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE is not allowed to withdraw USDe for stUSDe.
broken_invariants: The staking flow should preserve protocol accounting, authorization, and user fund invariants.
The withdrawal-flow flow should preserve protocol accounting, authorization, and user fund invariants.
The redemption-flow flow should preserve protocol accounting, authorization, and user fund invariants.
attack_preconditions: the affected code version and configuration match the public report
the report-described function sequence or state condition is reachable
attacker_capability: external user or protocol participant able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: observe accounting, access-control, or availability divergence

impact.description: A requirement is stated that a user with the SOFT_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE is not allowed to withdraw USDe for stUSDe.

why_not_higher: the final report classified this as Medium; impact or likelihood is bounded relative to High severity
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with concrete affected code paths and impact narrative
common_false_positive_angles: verify reachability under current roles, pause state, and integration configuration
check whether audited commit and line links match the code under review
check whether sponsor-side operational assumptions fully remove the exploit path
mitigations: With the function overriding present, to prevent the SOFT_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE from being able to exchange their stUSDs for USDe, make the following change in StakedUSDe https://github.com/code-423n4/2023-10-ethena/blob/ee67d9b542642c9757a6b826c82d0cae60256509/contracts/StakedUSDe.sol#L232 - if (hasRole(FULL_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE, caller) || hasRole(FULL_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE, receiver)) { + if (hasRole(FULL_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE, caller) || hasRole(FULL_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE, receiver) || hasRole(SOFT_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE, caller)) { revert OperationNotAllowed(); }
